kb1_adjustments: KB1 flag: duopoly_aon_marsh - AON has comparable scale in the same market.
dogma_adjustments: Rule 4: Highly quantified - $6.6B Risk & Insurance revenue, 22/23 consecutive quarters of revenue exceeding expense growth, 1300bps margin expansion since 2007.
Rule 3: AON is a pure competitor with comparable scale. Willis Towers Watson also competes. Scale advantage exists vs smaller brokers but not vs peers.
Rule 11: Industry structure fit - insurance brokerage has genuine scale economics in insurer access and data analytics, but the Big 3 brokers share this advantage.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Pattern I (quantitative differentiation) applies via the 22/23 quarters metric. The sustained operating leverage over nearly 6 years is a genuine Process Power indicator embedded in the Scale Economies claim. However, AON operates at comparable or larger scale. Score maintained at 0.5: real advantage but limited relative differentiation vs top-tier peers.
scored_claims.cagr_connection: Moderate: operating leverage model (revenue growing faster than costs in 22/23 quarters) should continue to expand margins as revenue grows. Insurance premium cycle and global risk complexity provide moderate CAGR support.

dogma_adjustments: Rule 4: 20 consecutive quarters of organic growth at Guy Carpenter is quantified evidence of client retention depth.
Rule 1: Advisory/analytics role at C-suite level (reinsurance companies 'come to rely very heavily') correctly describes a capability-based switching cost.
Rule 7: Guy Carpenter competes with Aon Re (Aon Benfield) and JLT Re. Relative retention vs these peers is not quantified.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Pattern I (quantitative differentiation) applies - 20 consecutive quarters is a well-quantified retention track record. The advisory role at C-suite level ('C-Suites come to rely very heavily') creates embedded relationship-based switching costs beyond pure transaction brokerage. The caveat is that Aon Benfield and JLT Re have comparable advisory capabilities. Score maintained at 0.5.
scored_claims.cagr_connection: Moderate: strong client retention at Guy Carpenter provides revenue visibility, and expanding advisory scope creates cross-sell opportunities. CAGR is supported by organic growth consistency.

dogma_adjustments: Rule 4: $136B AUM in investment consulting is quantified. 8% investments revenue growth.
Rule 12: Secondary call (Q2 2015) for Mercer Marketplace details.
Rule 3: Mercer competes with Aon Hewitt (benefits consulting) and Towers Watson in investment consulting. The $136B AUM is large but competitor AUM is not compared.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Pattern IV (structural market position) partially applies. $136B investment consulting AUM represents genuine scale advantage in providing institutional investment advice. Mercer Marketplace as a health exchange platform has early network characteristics. The secondary call context (Rule 12) applies to the Mercer Marketplace evidence. Score maintained at 0.5: solid institutional advisory scale without demonstrated peer superiority.
scored_claims.cagr_connection: Moderate: investment consulting AUM-based fees create steady recurring revenue. ACA complexity driving Mercer Marketplace adoption provides an incremental CAGR catalyst.

kb1_adjustments: KB1 flag: strategy_consulting_commoditization - Oliver Wyman competes with MBB (McKinsey, BCG, Bain).
dogma_adjustments: Rule 3: McKinsey, BCG, Bain have equivalent or stronger sector expertise in financial services and other industries Oliver Wyman targets.
Rule 4: Revenue growth rates (mid-teens 2014, 5% H1 2015) are quantified but not differentiated from MBB growth rates.
Rule 2: 'Accumulated proprietary expertise' is described as a result (revenue growth) rather than an independently describable capability.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Pattern B (industry common) and Pattern D (qualitative only) both apply. Strategy consulting expertise is an industry-wide attribute claimed by all major consulting firms. Oliver Wyman's financial services focus is notable but McKinsey and BCG have comparable or stronger financial services practices. Revenue growth is quantified but does not demonstrate relative advantage. Score maintained at 0.3.
scored_claims.cagr_connection: Weak: consulting revenue growth depends on project pipeline which is cyclical. No durable CAGR mechanism identified vs competitors.

dogma_adjustments: Rule 4: Historical EPS CAGR 13%/16%/15% over 3 years is verified financial performance. 13% long-term target is specific.
Rule 5: The CAGR mechanism (ACA complexity + global risk growth + operating leverage) has intermediate steps but each is credible.
Rule 11: Insurance industry and consulting industry structural trends provide genuine tailwinds.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Pattern I (quantitative differentiation) applies via 3-year historical EPS CAGR track record with specific numbers. The 13% long-term EPS CAGR target is grounded in demonstrated historical execution. ACA complexity and global risk growth are genuine structural tailwinds. Score maintained at 0.5: well-quantified CAGR claim with structural basis, but company-specific execution risk remains.
scored_claims.cagr_connection: Direct: 13% EPS CAGR target confirmed by 3-year track record (13%/16%/15%). Operating leverage (revenue > expense growth in 22/23 quarters) provides the mechanism.
